component.name: bulk
component.title: Bulk
component.description: Process collections of items in structured bulk operations with chunking, error thresholds, and watermark tracking.
component.label: core,scheduling

lazyStartProducer.label: producer
lazyStartProducer.description: Whether the producer should be started lazy (on the first message). By starting lazy you can use this to allow CamelContext and routes to startup in situations where a producer may otherwise fail during starting and cause the route to fail being started. By deferring this startup to be lazy then the startup failure can be handled during routing messages via Camel's routing error handlers. Beware that when the first message is processed then creating and starting the producer may take a little time and prolong the total processing time of the processing.

autowiredEnabled.label: advanced
autowiredEnabled.description: Whether autowiring is enabled. This is used for automatic autowiring options (the option must be marked as autowired) by looking up in the registry to find if there is a single instance of matching type, which then gets configured on the component. This can be used for automatic configuring JDBC data sources, JMS connection factories, AWS Clients, etc.

CamelBulkJobInstanceId.description: Unique identifier for this bulk job execution

CamelBulkAborted.description: Whether the bulk operation was aborted due to error threshold

CamelBulkIndex.description: Zero-based index of the current item

CamelBulkSize.description: Total number of items in the bulk operation

CamelBulkStepIndex.description: Index of the current step in a multi-step bulk operation

CamelBulkWatermarkValue.description: The current watermark value from the watermark store

acceptPolicy.description: Determines which items are eligible for processing in subsequent steps (after the first step). ALL processes all items, NO_FAILURES skips items that failed in a prior step, FAILURES_ONLY only processes items that failed (useful for error recovery steps).

aggregationStrategy.description: Optional bean reference to an AggregationStrategy for collecting results

errorThreshold.description: Fraction of failed items (0.0-1.0) before aborting the bulk operation. 1.0 means never abort. 0.1 means abort after 10% failures.

maxFailedRecords.description: Maximum number of failed records before aborting the bulk operation. -1 means disabled (use errorThreshold instead). 0 means abort on first failure.

onCompleteRef.description: Optional endpoint URI or bean reference called after the bulk operation completes, regardless of success or failure. Receives the BulkResult as the exchange body.

parallelProcessing.description: Process items within each chunk in parallel

processorRef.description: Bean reference to a Processor or endpoint URI that processes each individual item. Mutually exclusive with the steps option.

steps.description: Comma-separated list of endpoint URIs or bean references for multi-step bulk processing. Items flow through each step sequentially. Use with acceptPolicy to filter items between steps. Mutually exclusive with the processorRef option.

watermarkExpression.description: A Simple expression evaluated on each successfully processed item to extract a watermark value. The last non-null value is stored in the watermark store. When set, index-based watermark skipping is disabled and the current watermark value is set as a CamelBulkWatermarkValue header instead.

watermarkKey.description: Key used in the watermark store. Defaults to the job name.

watermarkStore.description: Optional bean reference to a Map for watermark tracking

lazyStartProducer.label: producer,advanced
lazyStartProducer.description: Whether the producer should be started lazy (on the first message). By starting lazy you can use this to allow CamelContext and routes to startup in situations where a producer may otherwise fail during starting and cause the route to fail being started. By deferring this startup to be lazy then the startup failure can be handled during routing messages via Camel's routing error handlers. Beware that when the first message is processed then creating and starting the producer may take a little time and prolong the total processing time of the processing.
